summary: The Elements of Statistical Learning by Hastie, Tibshirani, and Friedman presents key ideas in statistical learning within a common conceptual framework, emphasizing concepts over mathematics. The second edition covers both supervised and unsupervised learning, adding new chapters on random forests, ensemble methods, graphical models, and high-dimensional problems. It serves as a resource for statisticians and data mining practitioners across science and industry.
bullets: Covers supervised learning (prediction) and unsupervised learning in a unified statistical framework
Second edition adds chapters on random forests, ensemble learning, undirected graphical models, and high-dimensional (p>N) problems
Includes neural networks, SVMs, classification trees, boosting, and the LAR algorithm for the lasso
Emphasizes concepts and intuition over heavy mathematical theory, with many examples and color graphics
Authored by Stanford statistics professors known for GAMs, lasso, CART, MARS, and gradient boosting
